req.title: Account close (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A customer shall close an account with zero balance; closing with non-zero balance shall require sweep to another account. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-089 defines three distinct behavioural zones: (1) happy-path closure of a zero-balance account, (2) idempotent re-submission via the same Idempotency-Key, (3) rejection when balance is non-zero and no sweep target is supplied, and (4) the exact boundary where balance transitions from zero to one minor unit. I mapped these zones to five tests covering EP (normal close), STC (state idempotency), NEG (non-zero without sweep), BVA (balance == 1 minor unit), and DT (non-zero WITH sweep present). Each test relies only on narrow fixtures/helpers so that no test exceeds 40 code lines and all are self-contained.

tests.name: test_close_zero_balance_account_returns_200
preconditions: Account exists and is owned by the caller
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/close with valid auth token and a unique Idempotency-Key
trace: R-FIN-089 – 'A customer shall close an account with zero balance'
tests.executable:
def test_close_zero_balance_account_returns_200(api_client, zero_balance_account):
    account_id = zero_balance_account["id"]
    idempotency_key = <generate_idempotency_key>()
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/close",
        headers=headers,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["state"] == "closed"
    assert body.get("final_statement_url") is not None

tests.name: test_close_idempotent_duplicate_returns_original_result
preconditions: Account exists, is owned by the caller, and has zero balance
A unique Idempotency-Key is generated and stored
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/close with the SAME Idempotency-Key (duplicate submission)
expected: First call returns HTTP 200 with state == 'closed'
Second call returns HTTP 200 with identical body to first call
No duplicate final statement is generated
trace: R-FIN-089 – 'The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result'
tests.executable:
def test_close_idempotent_duplicate_returns_original_result(api_client, zero_balance_account):
    account_id = zero_balance_account["id"]
    idempotency_key = <generate_idempotency_key>()
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": idempotency_key}

    resp1 = api_client.post(f"/accounts/{account_id}/close", headers=headers)
    assert resp1.status_code == 200
    body1 = resp1.json()

    resp2 = api_client.post(f"/accounts/{account_id}/close", headers=headers)
    assert resp2.status_code == 200
    body2 = resp2.json()

    assert body1["state"] == "closed"
    assert body2["state"] == body1["state"]
    assert body2["final_statement_url"] == body1["final_statement_url"]
    assert body2.get("idempotency_key") == body1.get("idempotency_key")

tests.name: test_close_nonzero_balance_without_sweep_returns_409
preconditions: Account exists, is owned by the caller, and has a positive balance (e.g. 100.00)
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/close with valid auth token, unique Idempotency-Key, and NO sweep_to_account_id in body
expected: Response body contains error code indicating non-zero balance without sweep
trace: R-FIN-089 – 'Error pathways: 409 when balance != 0 without sweep'
R-FIN-089 – 'closing with non-zero balance shall require sweep to another account'
tests.executable:
def test_close_nonzero_balance_without_sweep_returns_409(api_client, nonzero_balance_account):
    account_id = nonzero_balance_account["id"]
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": <generate_idempotency_key>()}

    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/close",
        headers=headers,
        json={},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 409
    body = resp.json()
    assert "balance" in body.get("error", "").lower() or body.get("code") == "NONZERO_BALANCE"

    state_resp = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}")
    assert state_resp.json()["state"] != "closed"

tests.name: test_close_balance_one_minor_unit_without_sweep_returns_409
preconditions: Account exists, is owned by the caller, and has a balance of exactly 0.01 (one minor currency unit)
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/close with valid auth token, unique Idempotency-Key, and NO sweep_to_account_id
expected: HTTP 409 Conflict (balance 0.01 is non-zero, so sweep is mandatory)
trace: R-FIN-089 – BVA: boundary between zero-balance (allowed) and non-zero balance (requires sweep)
R-FIN-089 – 'closing with non-zero balance shall require sweep to another account'
tests.executable:
def test_close_balance_one_minor_unit_without_sweep_returns_409(api_client, account_with_balance):
    account_id = account_with_balance(balance="0.01")["id"]
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": <generate_idempotency_key>()}

    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/close",
        headers=headers,
        json={},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 409
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = body.get("error", "").lower()
    assert "balance" in error_text or body.get("code") == "NONZERO_BALANCE"

    state_resp = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}")
    assert state_resp.json()["state"] != "closed"

tests.name: test_close_nonzero_balance_with_valid_sweep_returns_200
preconditions: Source account exists, is owned by the caller, and has a positive balance
Destination account exists, is owned by the same caller, and is open
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/close with valid auth token, unique Idempotency-Key, and sweep_to_account_id pointing to destination account
expected: Destination account balance increased by the swept amount
trace: R-FIN-089 – 'closing with non-zero balance shall require sweep to another account'
DT: condition combination (balance > 0) AND (sweep_target supplied) => close succeeds
tests.executable:
def test_close_nonzero_balance_with_valid_sweep_returns_200(
    api_client, nonzero_balance_account, sweep_destination_account
):
    source_id = nonzero_balance_account["id"]
    dest_id = sweep_destination_account["id"]
    original_source_balance = nonzero_balance_account["balance"]

    dest_before = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{dest_id}").json()["balance"]

    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": <generate_idempotency_key>()}
    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{source_id}/close",
        headers=headers,
        json={"sweep_to_account_id": dest_id},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["state"] == "closed"
    assert body.get("final_statement_url") is not None

    dest_after = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{dest_id}").json()["balance"]
    assert float(dest_after) == float(dest_before) + float(original_source_balance)
